title: VPN for Gaming: Does It Help Ping, or Just Add Lag?
description: VPNs are marketed for gaming but their actual effect on ping and latency depends entirely on where the VPN server is relative to the game server. Here is what you need to know before enabling one.
content:
The Honest Answer About VPNs and Ping

For most gamers, a VPN makes ping worse, not better. Adding any intermediary between you and the game server increases the number of network hops your data travels, which increases latency. If your connection to a game server is already optimal (direct, no routing inefficiencies), a VPN only adds overhead.

When a VPN Can Improve Gaming Performance

There are specific cases where a VPN helps. ISP throttling: if your internet provider detects gaming traffic and rate-limits it, routing through a VPN can bypass that throttling. Some ISPs do this, particularly during peak hours. Route optimization: if your ISP's routing to a specific game server is inefficient (taking a longer geographic path than necessary), a VPN server that connects more directly to the game server can reduce latency. You need to test this with and without the VPN on the specific server region. DDoS protection: competitive gamers who stream or have a public profile are sometimes targeted with denial-of-service attacks on their IP address. A VPN masks your real IP from opponents.

How to Test if a VPN Helps You

Enable your in-game ping display (most games have this). Note your baseline ping to each server region. Enable the VPN and connect to a server in the same region as the game server. Compare ping values. If ping goes up, the VPN is not helping for that server. If ping goes down, the VPN is improving your route. Test several VPN server locations, not just one, as they differ significantly in their routing quality.

Best VPNs for Gaming

ExpressVPN and NordVPN have the most extensive server networks, which increases the chance of finding a server that improves your route to a specific game server. Mullvad is fast but has fewer servers. For gaming specifically, server network size matters more than it does for privacy use cases. WireGuard protocol (available on NordVPN, Mullvad, and others) has significantly lower overhead than OpenVPN or IKEv2 and is the recommended protocol for gaming.
